page: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v7 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Dadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs operating at 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes a 3780 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No dedicated GPUs are present on this server type.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for single-core stress-ng tests, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark extended instructions. Multi-core processing and memory bandwidth tests, including both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads or writes, consistently rank in the top 25%. Cryptographic and compression workloads also show strong results, with single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reaching the top 10%. For database operations, the instance achieves top-tier PassMark database scores and strong Redis SET performance.

The hardware configuration and benchmark profile make this instance suitable for database operations, web serving, and medium-scale language model inference. While prompt processing and text generation for small and medium models rank in the top 10%, performance drops to the middle 50% for large 70B models due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. Qualitatively, the combination of a high-frequency dedicated CPU, substantial local storage, and a balanced memory-to-core ratio offers high resource density and compute efficiency for enterprise workloads without the added cost of specialized accelerator hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dadsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs. It features 256.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and 3780 GB of local storage, with Premium Storage capabilities and no GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-core processing, PassMark database operations, and small-to-medium LLM inference, alongside strong multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. This instance is suited for database hosting, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v7 is a Dadsv7 virtual machine featuring a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC processor, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and single-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v7 is a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC virtual machine with 256 GB RAM and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for database and web workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency 64-vCPU AMD EPYC virtual machine with balanced memory and substantial local storage for demanding database and compute workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory and 3780 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core, database, and small-model LLM inference performance
Strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth capabilities
Suited for database operations, web serving, and CPU-based inference
